skill.name: solid-principles
skill.description: SOLID object-oriented design principles for maintainable code
skill.summary: SOLID object-oriented design principles for maintainable code
skill.author: Benny9193

security_audit.summary: Static command, weak-crypto, database-file, and reconnaissance findings were reviewed and dismissed as false positives from Markdown, TypeScript examples, and prose. No executable scripts, network calls, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found. The skill has a medium content risk because a positive dependency inversion example still shows SQL built with interpolated input.

medium_findings.title: Unsafe SQL Interpolation in Teaching Example
medium_findings.description: The dependency inversion section presents a repository example that builds SQL by inserting the id value into a query string. Line 219 appears in a BAD example, but line 240 appears after the GOOD heading and may teach SQL injection-prone database access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The unsafe pattern is directly visible in the documented TypeScript examples. It is not executable malware, but it can mislead users into copying an insecure query style.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Findings Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported shell backticks are Markdown code fences and TypeScript template literals, not command execution. The weak cryptography, SQLite database file, and system reconnaissance alerts map to ordinary prose or example database code, with no executable behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the flagged locations shows documentation examples only. No shell invocation, filesystem probing, cryptographic operation, or SQLite database file access is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: String-Interpolated SQL Query
dangerous_patterns.description: A sample repository method constructs a SQL query by embedding an id parameter directly into the SQL text. The example should use parameterized queries before publication.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The query construction is visible in the skill content and uses direct string interpolation. The risk is educational content risk rather than active code execution.

content.user_title: Apply SOLID Principles to Object-Oriented Code
content.value_statement: Object-oriented code can become hard to test, extend, and reason about. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical SOLID guidance with TypeScript examples.
actual_capabilities: Explains all five SOLID principles with concise definitions.
Shows before-and-after TypeScript examples for common design problems.
Maps each principle to practical code smells.
Provides guidance on when to apply or avoid SOLID refactoring.
Includes a checklist for reviewing object-oriented designs.
limitations: It is a documentation-only skill and does not inspect repositories automatically.
Examples focus on TypeScript-style object-oriented code.
It does not provide framework-specific architecture patterns.
One database example should be revised to use parameterized queries.

use_cases.title: Review a Service Class
use_cases.description: Identify responsibilities that should move into smaller services or collaborators.

use_cases.title: Teach SOLID in Code Reviews
use_cases.description: Use short examples to explain why a design is hard to extend or substitute.

use_cases.title: Plan a Refactor
use_cases.description: Choose which SOLID principle addresses repeated conditionals, broad interfaces, or tight coupling.

prompt_templates.title: Explain a Principle
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the Single Responsibility Principle using a small TypeScript service example. Keep the explanation practical.

prompt_templates.title: Find SOLID Violations
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this class for SOLID violations. Name the principle, explain the problem, and suggest a focused refactor.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Refactor Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare two ways to refactor this payment workflow using the Open/Closed Principle and Dependency Inversion Principle.

prompt_templates.title: Design an Extension Point
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an extensible object-oriented structure for this feature. Apply SOLID principles without adding unnecessary abstractions.

output_examples.input: A UserService creates users, sends email, validates addresses, and builds reports.
output_examples.output: The response separates user creation, validation, email delivery, and reporting. It explains that each class now has one clear reason to change.

output_examples.input: A payment processor uses conditionals for credit card, PayPal, and crypto payments.
output_examples.output: The response recommends a payment method abstraction. New payment types can be added without modifying the processor.

output_examples.input: A robot class implements eating, sleeping, meeting, and reporting methods it cannot support.
output_examples.output: The response splits the broad worker interface into focused capabilities. Robot classes depend only on methods they can actually provide.

best_practices: Apply SOLID when change, testing, or substitution problems are visible.
Keep examples grounded in the current codebase and its existing abstractions.
Prefer small refactors that remove a clear maintenance problem.
anti_patterns: Do not split code into many tiny classes without a real reason.
Do not hide simple logic behind unnecessary interfaces.
Do not copy database examples without adding safe parameter handling.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my code automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides design guidance and examples for Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to apply during a task.

faq.question: Which languages does it support best?
faq.answer: The examples use TypeScript, but the principles apply to many object-oriented languages.

faq.question: Can it help with code reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It links common code smells to specific SOLID principles and refactoring options.

faq.question: Is this only for large systems?
faq.answer: No. It is most useful when changes are becoming hard, tests are difficult, or abstractions are already needed.

faq.question: Does it cover functional programming?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on object-oriented design principles and related class or interface structures.

faq.question: Are the examples production-ready?
faq.answer: They are teaching examples. Database examples should be adapted with parameterized queries and project-specific safety rules.
